-{"id":"bd-32lz3","title":"[Maintenance] Design shutdown coordinator + API contract","description":"## Goal

Define a concrete design for graceful shutdown + maintenance/drain mode:

- State machine (`running` / `draining` / `stopped`)
- Transition triggers (admin API, SIGINT/SIGTERM)
- Deadline semantics (hard cutoff vs best-effort)
- Error taxonomy for blocked operations
- WebSocket behavior (event, close code, reconnect hints)

## Deliverables

- Proposed module/API surface:
  - `ShutdownCoordinator` interface
  - `withMaintenanceGuard(...)` / middleware integration points
- Route spec:
  - `GET /system/maintenance`
  - `POST /system/maintenance`
  - response JSON shapes + OpenAPI updates
- Standard error codes (example):
  - `MAINTENANCE_MODE` (503)
  - `DRAINING` (503)
- WS close code + reason mapping (documented constants)

## Constraints

- Must respect existing auth model (admin-only when auth enabled).
- Must not introduce new long-lived background loops beyond what's needed.
- Must use structured logging (no ad-hoc console logs).

## Test Plan Notes

Document the test strategy so implementation tasks can lift it directly.","design":"## State Machine

Introduce a single in-memory coordinator that represents the gateway lifecycle mode.

- `running`: normal operation
- `maintenance`: operator-requested maintenance/drain mode (read-only allowed, mutating ops blocked)
- `draining`: shutdown-in-progress (subset of maintenance with a deadline)
- `stopped`: optional terminal marker (primarily for observability/logging)

In practice, implementation can treat `maintenance` and `draining` as the only non-running modes.

## Coordinator API (Proposed)

Location: `apps/gateway/src/services/maintenance.service.ts`

- `getMaintenanceState(): MaintenanceState`
- `getMaintenanceSnapshot(): MaintenanceSnapshot` (JSON-friendly)
- `enterMaintenance({ reason?, actor? })`
- `exitMaintenance({ actor? })`
- `startDraining({ deadlineSeconds, reason?, actor? })`
- `getRetryAfterSeconds(): number | null`
- `trackHttpRequestStart()` / `trackHttpRequestEnd()` for in-flight request counts

The coordinator must be synchronous + atomic (no awaits between check and state mutation).

## HTTP Guarding (Mutating Ops)

Add a global Hono middleware:

Location: `apps/gateway/src/middleware/maintenance.ts`

- Block mutating methods: `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE` when mode != `running`
- Explicit allowlist (always allowed):
  - `/health`
  - `/system/maintenance` (so operators can disable maintenance)
- Response:
  - status: `503`
  - error codes (add to taxonomy):
    - `MAINTENANCE_MODE`
    - `DRAINING`
  - include `Retry-After` header when a deadline exists
  - include error `details`: `{ mode, deadlineAt, retryAfterSeconds }`

Note: We intentionally keep read-only routes available during maintenance/drain.

## Maintenance API Contract

Add admin-only endpoints under existing `apps/gateway/src/routes/system.ts`:

- `GET /system/maintenance`
  - returns current coordinator state + progress counters

- `POST /system/maintenance` body:
  - `{ enabled: boolean, deadlineSeconds?: number, reason?: string }`
  - semantics:
    - `enabled=false` => transition to `running`
    - `enabled=true` and `deadlineSeconds` present => transition to `draining`
    - `enabled=true` and no deadline => transition to `maintenance`

Response should include:

- `mode`
- `startedAt` / `deadlineAt`
- `retryAfterSeconds`
- counters:
  - `http.inflightRequests`
  - `ws.activeConnections`

## WebSocket Behavior

- Deny new upgrades when mode != `running` (return HTTP 503 for upgrade request).
- On entering maintenance/draining:
  - publish a deterministic WS event on a new channel `system:maintenance`
    - add channel type in `apps/gateway/src/ws/channels.ts`
    - add message type `system.maintenance` in `apps/gateway/src/ws/messages.ts`
  - close existing sockets with a stable close code + reason.

Close codes:

- Prefer standard codes:
  - `1013` (Try Again Later) for maintenance
  - `1012` (Service Restart) for draining/shutdown

Expose these as constants (avoid magic numbers).

## Shutdown Signal Hook (Design Only)

Implementation belongs to `bd-1prjp`, but design decision:

- On SIGINT/SIGTERM:
  - transition to `draining` with a configurable deadline (default 30s)
  - stop periodic background loops (where stop functions exist)
  - stop job worker (await bounded stop)
  - close WS connections
  - exit after deadline

## Tests

- Unit: coordinator transitions, deadline math, Retry-After formatting
- Integration:
  - enable maintenance -> mutating endpoint returns 503 + correct error payload
  - WS upgrade rejected during maintenance
  - maintenance enable publishes event then closes connections","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:37.605436723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:51:03.423884327Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend","ops"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32lz3","depends_on_id":"bd-1vdtm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:37.619755892Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

## Coordinator API (Proposed)

- `getMaintenanceState(): MaintenanceState`

The coordinator must be synchronous + atomic (no awaits between check and state mutation).

## HTTP Guarding (Mutating Ops)

Add a global Hono middleware:

Location: `apps/gateway/src/middleware/maintenance.ts`

- Block mutating methods: `POST`, `PUT`, `PATCH`, `DELETE` when mode != `running`

  - `/health`

- Response:
  - status: `503`
  - error codes (add to taxonomy):
    - `MAINTENANCE_MODE`
    - `DRAINING`
  - include `Retry-After` header when a deadline exists
  - include error `details`: `{ mode, deadlineAt, retryAfterSeconds }`

Note: We intentionally keep read-only routes available during maintenance/drain.

## Maintenance API Contract

Add admin-only endpoints under existing `apps/gateway/src/routes/system.ts`:

- `GET /system/maintenance`

    - `enabled=false` => transition to `running`

    - `enabled=true` and no deadline => transition to `maintenance`

- `mode`

  - close existing sockets with a stable close code + reason.

- Prefer standard codes:
  - `1013` (Try Again Later) for maintenance
  - `1012` (Service Restart) for draining/shutdown

Expose these as constants (avoid magic numbers).

Implementation belongs to `bd-1prjp`, but design decision:

- On SIGINT/SIGTERM:
  - transition to `draining` with a configurable deadline (default 30s)
  - stop periodic background loops (where stop functions exist)
  - stop job worker (await bounded stop)
  - close WS connections
  - exit after deadline

## Tests

- Unit: coordinator transitions, deadline math, Retry-After formatting
- Integration:
  - enable maintenance -> mutating endpoint returns 503 + correct error payload
  - WS upgrade rejected during maintenance
  - maintenance enable publishes event then closes connections","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:37.605436723Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T06:01:44.999549913Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T06:01:44.999474383Z","close_reason":"Completed: design notes captured","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend","ops"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32lz3","depends_on_id":"bd-1vdtm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:37.619755892Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-9mcg1","title":"[Maintenance] Implement HTTP maintenance guard + status endpoints","description":"## Scope

- Implement `ShutdownCoordinator` (in-memory) per design.
- Add admin-only routes:
  - `GET /system/maintenance`
  - `POST /system/maintenance`
- Add a guard for mutating routes:
  - during maintenance/draining, return 503 with a stable error body
  - include `retryAfterSeconds` when a deadline exists

## Notes

- Prefer a middleware approach so we can apply policy consistently and avoid route-by-route drift.
- Exemptions:
  - `/health` should remain available
  - documentation endpoints can be configurable (default allow read-only)

## Logging

- On maintenance state change: log one structured line with { enabled, mode, deadlineAt, reason, actor }.
- On blocked request: log at `info` (not error) with route + auth subject.

## Acceptance

- Mutating endpoints are blocked during maintenance/draining.
- Status endpoint reflects current state and progress counters.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:50.847128606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T05:51:17.755169695Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9mcg1","depends_on_id":"bd-1vdtm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:50.864709306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-9mcg1","depends_on_id":"bd-32lz3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:15:43.653976774Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- On blocked request: log at `info` (not error) with route + auth subject.

## Acceptance

- Mutating endpoints are blocked during maintenance/draining.
- Status endpoint reflects current state and progress counters.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:50.847128606Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T06:02:09.074453868Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T06:02:09.074375783Z","close_reason":"Completed: maintenance middleware + /system/maintenance endpoints + tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-9mcg1","depends_on_id":"bd-1vdtm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:14:50.864709306Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-9mcg1","depends_on_id":"bd-32lz3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T05:15:43.653976774Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
